risk_summary: Early usage is concentrated in field reporting while project management workflows remain inconsistent across active jobs.
recommendation_1: Identify three lighthouse projects and publish role-specific adoption targets.
recommendation_2: Run superintendent mobile enablement sessions for foremen and assistant supers.
recommendation_3: Create a weekly review for overdue submittals and stalled RFI responses.

risk_summary: Mobile engagement improved, but office-side document workflows are not yet part of the project cadence.
recommendation_1: Pair project engineers with high-adoption superintendents for two reporting cycles.
recommendation_2: Add RFI and submittal aging views to the weekly operations meeting.
recommendation_3: Review inactive licenses before the January project launches.

risk_summary: New project launches created more daily log activity, but submittal response ownership is still unclear.
recommendation_1: Assign a document-control owner for projects over $20M.
recommendation_2: Build saved views for overdue submittals and RFIs by project manager.
recommendation_3: Make role-based onboarding required for all new active projects.

risk_summary: Adoption moved into healthier territory, but usage is uneven between highway and vertical project teams.
recommendation_1: Run adoption score reviews by project type and region.
recommendation_2: Have project executives sponsor the two lowest-usage teams.
recommendation_3: Create a 30-day submittal response sprint with PM leadership.

risk_summary: RFI consistency improved, but subcontractor-facing document turnaround is still a schedule risk.
recommendation_1: Standardize RFI and submittal saved views for every project kickoff.
recommendation_2: Review subcontractor enablement gaps with field leadership.
recommendation_3: Publish a weekly adoption leaderboard for active projects.

risk_summary: The rollout is gaining momentum, but usage may plateau without executive governance and clear workflow owners.
recommendation_1: Create a monthly executive adoption checkpoint with project sponsors.
recommendation_2: Tie submittal response targets to project health reviews.
recommendation_3: Refresh inactive-user cleanup before the next licensing review.

risk_summary: Adoption is approaching healthy levels, but new project starts need a repeatable launch motion.
recommendation_1: Codify the launch checklist for Procore workflows on every new project.
recommendation_2: Assign one accountable owner for each workflow metric in the next 60 days.
recommendation_3: Use coaching office hours for teams below the adoption benchmark.

risk_summary: Adoption is healthy but still at risk of backsliding because submittal habits and new-project onboarding are not fully standardized.
recommendation_1: Set a 90-day adoption governance cadence with executive sponsorship.
recommendation_2: Launch a submittal response improvement plan for the five highest-volume projects.
recommendation_3: Create role-based onboarding paths for project managers, superintendents, and project engineers.
